title: Design System Glossary
content: Design System Glossary Home Courses Articles Books Templates Glossary About Menu Menu # Glossary Design systems can be tricky if you don’t know what certain words mean! Here’s a quick primer to help you get acclimated. # Glossary Design systems can be tricky if you don’t know what certain words mean! Here’s a quick primer to help you get acclimated. # Glossary Design systems can be tricky if you don’t know what certain words mean! Here’s a quick primer to help you get acclimated. Component Component Component A reusable snippet of code. Sometimes used interchangeably with pattern [./glossary#pattern], but we think of them as different things. A reusable snippet of code. Sometimes used interchangeably with pattern [./glossary#pattern], but we think of them as different things. Component Library Component Library Component Library One of two main ingredients of any design system—the other being guidelines [./glossary#guidelines]—a component library—also referred to as a pattern library—is a collection of components [./glossary#component] or patterns [./glossary#pattern] where you can browse examples of components and find code references for how to implement the components into a codebase. Examples of component libraries include Storybook [https://storybook.js.org/], Pattern Lab [https://patternlab.io/], and Fractal [https://fractal.build/]. Further Reading: Pattern Library First: An Approach For Managing CSS [https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2018/07/pattern-library-first-css/] by Rachel Andrew One of two main ingredients of any design system—the other being guidelines [./glossary#guidelines]—a component library—also referred to as a pattern library—is a collection of components [./glossary#component] or patterns [./glossary#pattern] where you can browse examples of components and find code references for how to implement the components into a codebase. Examples of component libraries include Storybook [https://storybook.js.org/], Pattern Lab [https://patternlab.io/], and Fractal [https://fractal.build/]. Further Reading: Pattern Library First: An Approach For Managing CSS [https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2018/07/pattern-library-first-css/] by Rachel Andrew Design System Design System Design System The smallest set of components [./glossary#component] and guidelines [./glossary#guidelines] your organization needs to make your digital products consistently, efficiently, and quickly. Many people have different and useful definitions for design systems: Designer Alla Kholmatova [https://www.smashingmagazine.com/design-systems-book/] calls design systems “a set of connected patterns and shared practices, coherently organized to serve the purposes of a digital product.” Designer/developer Sylvee L. says design systems are [https://medium.muz.li/what-is-a-design-system-1e43d19e7696] “collections of rules, constraints, and principles, implemented in design and code.” Designer Nathan Curtis says [https://medium.com/eightshapes-llc/defining-design-systems-6dd4b03e0ff6], “A design system offers a library of visual style, components, and other concerns documented and released by an individual, team or community as code and design tools so that adopting products can be more efficient and cohesive,” among other things. Developer Jeremy Keith says [https://adactio.com/journal/13844], “I think we lose sight of the real value of a design system when we focus too much on the components. The components are the trees. The design system is the forest.” Front-end designer Brad Frost says [http://bradfrost.com/blog/link/design-systems/], “A design system [is] the official story of how your organization designs and builds digital products.” The smallest set of components [./glossary#component] and guidelines [./glossary#guidelines] your organization needs to make your digital products consistently, efficiently, and quickly. Many people have different and useful definitions for design systems: Designer Alla Kholmatova [https://www.smashingmagazine.com/design-systems-book/] calls design systems “a set of connected patterns and shared practices, coherently organized to serve the purposes of a digital product.” Designer/developer Sylvee L. says design systems are [https://medium.muz.li/what-is-a-design-system-1e43d19e7696] “collections of rules, constraints, and principles, implemented in design and code.” Designer Nathan Curtis says [https://medium.com/eightshapes-llc/defining-design-systems-6dd4b03e0ff6], “A design system offers a library of visual style, components, and other concerns documented and released by an individual, team or community as code and design tools so that adopting products can be more efficient and cohesive,” among other things. Developer Jeremy Keith says [https://adactio.com/journal/13844], “I think we lose sight of the real value of a design system when we focus too much on the components. The components are the trees. The design system is the forest.” Front-end designer Brad Frost says [http://bradfrost.com/blog/link/design-systems/], “A design system [is] the official story of how your organization designs and builds digital products.” Governance Governance Governance The particular approach your organization uses to keep its design system up-to-date. Further Reading: Team Models for Scaling a Design System [https://medium.com/eightshapes-llc/team-models-for-scaling-a-design-system-2cf9d03be6a0] by Nathan Curtis The Salesforce Team Model for Scaling a Design System [https://medium.com/salesforce-ux/the-salesforce-team-model-for-scaling-a-design-system-d89c2a2d404b] by Jina Anne Episode 6 – Maintaining and Evolving Your Design System [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ejhk8tDO5fU] by InVision The particular approach your organization uses to keep its design system up-to-date. Further Reading: Team Models for Scaling a Design System [https://medium.com/eightshapes-llc/team-models-for-scaling-a-design-system-2cf9d03be6a0] by Nathan Curtis The Salesforce Team Model for Scaling a Design System [https://medium.com/salesforce-ux/the-salesforce-team-model-for-scaling-a-design-system-d89c2a2d404b] by Jina Anne Episode 6 – Maintaining and Evolving Your Design System [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ejhk8tDO5fU] by InVision Guidelines Guidelines Guidelines The other big ingredient of design systems (along with a component library [./glossary#component-library]), guidelines are suggestions or rules to follow for how to best use any given design system. The other big ingredient of design systems (along with a component library [./glossary#component-library]), guidelines are suggestions or rules to follow for how to best use any given design system. Pattern Pattern Pattern A reusable flow, like signing in or checking out. Sometimes used interchangeably with component [./glossary#component], but we think of them as different things. A reusable flow, like signing in or checking out. Sometimes used interchangeably with component [./glossary#component], but we think of them as different things. Pilot Pilot Pilot A product designed to be a proof-of-concept for the efficacy of a design system. Further Reading: Design Systems: Pilots & Scorecards [https://danmall.com/posts/design-systems-pilots-scorecards/] by Dan Mall A product designed to be a proof-of-concept for the efficacy of a design system. Further Reading: Design Systems: Pilots & Scorecards [https://danmall.com/posts/design-systems-pilots-scorecards/] by Dan Mall Reference Site Reference Site Reference Site A visible manifestation of a design system, typically a URL that displays both the full component library [./glossary#component-library] and a set of guidelines [./glossary#guidelines]. A visible manifestation of a design system, typically a URL that displays both the full component library [./glossary#component-library] and a set of guidelines [./glossary#guidelines]. Token Token Token A platform- and technology-agnostic way to store a value to be ingested by specific applications. Examples of useful properties to store as tokens: color palette, typographic families, spacing units. Further Reading: What are Design Tokens? [https://css-tricks.com/what-are-design-tokens/] by Robin Rendle Tokens in Design Systems [https://medium.com/eightshapes-llc/tokens-in-design-systems-25dd82d58421] by Nathan Curtis A platform- and technology-agnostic way to store a value to be ingested by specific applications. Examples of useful properties to store as tokens: color palette, typographic families, spacing units. Further Reading: What are Design Tokens? [https://css-tricks.com/what-are-design-tokens/] by Robin Rendle Tokens in Design Systems [https://medium.com/eightshapes-llc/tokens-in-design-systems-25dd82d58421] by Nathan Curtis UI Kit UI Kit UI Kit As it relates to a design system, a UI Kit is a representation of coded components [./glossary#component] created in a way that designers who don’t know code can create interface mockups. Sketch libraries and Figma design systems [https://www.figma.com/blog/how-to-build-your-design-system-in-figma/] are examples of UI kits. As it relates to a design system, a UI Kit is a representation of coded components [./glossary#component] created in a way that designers who don’t know code can create interface mockups. Sketch libraries and Figma design systems [https://www.figma.com/blog/how-to-build-your-design-system-in-figma/] are examples of UI kits.
